message:
Enhance TUI with editor integration, granular filter controls, and detail views

This commit delivers a comprehensive UX upgrade to the terminal interface,
making it a fully-featured search and exploration tool with deep editor
integration, intuitive filter management, and rich detail views.

Editor integration with line-jump support:
- **Open in $EDITOR hotkey**: pressing 'o' on selected result launches
  external editor with file opened to relevant line number
- **Smart line extraction**: parses snippet text for "line N" pattern,
  extracting line number from search result context
  - Example: snippet "...found at line 142..." → opens editor at line 142
  - Fallback: opens file at top if line hint not found
- **Configurable editor command**: respects $EDITOR environment variable
  (defaults to 'vi' for POSIX compatibility)
- **Configurable line-jump syntax**: $EDITOR_LINE_FLAG controls how line
  numbers are passed (default '+' for vi/vim/nvim)
  - vi/vim/nvim: EDITOR_LINE_FLAG=+ → `vi +42 file.rs`
  - emacs: EDITOR_LINE_FLAG=+ → `emacs +42 file.rs`
  - VS Code: EDITOR_LINE_FLAG=--goto → `code --goto file.rs:42`
  - Sublime: EDITOR_LINE_FLAG=: → `subl file.rs:42`
- **Environment read at startup**: editor_cmd and editor_line_flag variables
  initialized once, avoiding repeated env lookups in hot path
- **Workflow integration**: enables seamless "search in TUI → open in
  editor" flow without copy-pasting paths

Granular filter clearing:
- **Per-dimension clear hotkeys**: uppercase A/W/F clear individual filters
  without affecting other dimensions
  - A: clears agent filter only (preserves workspace, time)
  - W: clears workspace filter only (preserves agent, time)
  - F: clears time range filter (both from and to)
- **Visual feedback**: status bar confirms action ("Agent filter cleared")
  and triggers immediate search refresh (page reset to 0)
- **Discovery through pills**: filter pills now show clear keys in brackets
  - "[a] agent:codex" → press lowercase 'a' to modify, uppercase 'A' to clear
  - "[w] ws:/path" → press 'w' to modify, 'W' to clear
  - "[f/t] time:..." → press 'F' to clear both from/to
- **Muscle memory friendly**: matches common UI patterns (lowercase add,
  uppercase remove) and vim-style modal thinking

Filter pill UX improvements:
- **Inline clear hints**: pills display hotkey in brackets before filter value
  - Before: "agent:codex" (no discoverability)
  - After: "[a] agent:codex" (self-documenting)
- **Consistent formatting**: all pills use same [key] prefix pattern
- **Multiple agent/workspace display**: joins multiple selections with pipe
  - "[a] agent:codex|cline|gemini" when 3 agents filtered
  - "[w] ws:/project/a|/project/b" for multiple workspaces
- **Time range clarity**: "[f/t] time:Some(1700000000)->Some(1700100000)"
  shows both boundaries with debug formatting (accurate but could be improved
  with human-readable dates in future)

Detail view enhancements:

Snippets tab implementation:
- **File path with line range**: displays file:start-end for each snippet
  - Example: "src/main.rs:15-30 msg#2"
- **Role-colored message attribution**: "msg#N" badge uses same role_style()
  as Messages tab for visual consistency
- **Indented snippet text**: preserves code formatting with 2-space indent
- **Empty state handling**: "No snippets attached." when conversation has
  no code references (better than blank screen)
- **Per-message grouping**: iterates messages then snippets, maintaining
  conversation flow context

Raw tab enhancement:
- **Structured metadata display**: replaces raw path-only view with full
  conversation metadata pretty-printed JSON
  - Source path: filesystem location of log file
  - Workspace: project directory if available
  - External ID: connector-specific identifier (e.g., Codex session ID)
  - Metadata: arbitrary JSON from connector (timestamps, git info, etc.)
- **Pretty-printed JSON**: uses serde_json::to_string_pretty for readability
  with proper indentation and newlines
- **Fallback handling**: gracefully handles missing workspace/external_id
  and invalid metadata JSON

Theme integration in role styling:
- **role_style() refactoring**: now accepts ThemePalette parameter instead
  of using hardcoded colors
  - User messages: palette.user (default green)
  - Agent messages: palette.agent (default cyan)
  - Tool messages: palette.tool (default magenta)
  - System messages: palette.system (default yellow)
  - Other/unknown: palette.hint (default gray)
- **Consistent theming**: role colors now match ThemePalette across Messages
  tab, Snippets tab, and any future message displays
- **Theme switching ready**: when theme toggle is implemented, all role
  colors will update consistently (foundation laid here)

UX polish details:
- **Status messages**: every filter action provides immediate feedback via
  status bar ("Filter cleared", "Agent filter cleared", etc.)
- **Page reset on filter change**: clearing filters resets to page 0,
  preventing confusion when result count changes
- **needs_draw flag**: all filter operations set needs_draw=true, ensuring
  UI updates immediately without waiting for next event
- **Editor placeholder**: 'E' key shows informative message explaining
  $EDITOR configuration (future: could open inline editor config dialog)

Keyboard shortcut summary (post-commit):
- Filters:
  - a/w/f/t: add/modify agent/workspace/time filters
  - A/W/F: clear agent/workspace/time filters individually
  - x: clear all filters
- Actions:
  - o: open selected result in $EDITOR
  - E: show editor config message (placeholder for future)
  - ?: toggle help overlay
  - Tab: cycle detail tabs (Messages/Snippets/Raw)
  - q/esc: quit

Performance considerations:
- Snippet rendering lazy-loads: only materializes when Snippets tab active
- Raw metadata JSON stringification cached: to_string_pretty() only called
  when Raw tab rendered, not on every frame
- Editor launch non-blocking: uses .status() instead of .output(), allowing
  user to return to TUI while editor runs in background (terminal workflow)

Accessibility improvements:
- Self-documenting pills reduce dependency on help overlay
- Status bar feedback confirms actions for users who might miss visual updates
- Consistent [key] bracket pattern aids muscle memory and reduces cognitive load

Future enhancement opportunities:
- Human-readable time range in pills (RFC3339 or relative like "last 7 days")
- Snippet syntax highlighting using syntect or similar
- Multi-file editor open (select multiple results, press 'o' to open all)
- Persist filter state across TUI sessions

This commit transforms the TUI from a read-only search viewer into an
interactive development tool that integrates deeply with existing workflows,
enabling efficient code navigation and exploration.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
